title: dreaming_pass event (stderr structured JSON)
description: Machine-readable contract for the single-line JSON event emitted on stderr by `mengdie dream` (decay-pass output). Producer: `src/bin/cli.rs::format_structured_json`. Consumers: `scripts/verify-decay.sh` (operator approval gate until BL-010 daemon lands), and any future daemon/analytics tooling. Locked by plan 015.

schema_version.description: Contract version. See Bump Rules below.

event.description: Event discriminator. Fixed string — do NOT rename without bumping schema_version.

promoted.description: Short-term memories promoted to long-term in this pass.

demoted.description: Long-term memories demoted (live mode); always 0 in --decay-dry-run mode.

decay_floor_breaches.description: Count of long-term memories whose effective_relevance fell below the decay floor (0.20). Equals `breaches.length`.

avg_effective_before.description: Mean effective_relevance across long-term memories BEFORE the decay pass. 0.0 if corpus contains no long-term rows with non-null last_recalled (null-guard at src/core/dreaming.rs:203-206).

avg_effective_after.description: Mean effective_relevance AFTER the decay pass (identical to before in --decay-dry-run).

dry_run.description: True when invoked via --decay-dry-run; operator's first-run procedure always sets this.

items.description: Memory ID (UUID v4) of a breached long-term memory.

breaches.description: IDs of breached memories. Length invariant: equals decay_floor_breaches.

$comment: BUMP RULES (plan 015, expanded per plan-015 review P2.5): Bump `schema_version` when ANY of: (a) a required field is removed; (b) a field is renamed; (c) the semantics or units of an existing field change; (d) a field's TYPE is narrowed or widened (e.g., integer → number; string → object; scalar → array) — type drift is semantically breaking even when the name is preserved; (e) a structural INVARIANT between fields changes (e.g., the `breaches.length == decay_floor_breaches` guarantee is loosened or tightened); (f) a new REQUIRED field is added (strictly-additive optional fields do NOT bump). Consumers ignore unknown keys by default, so strictly-additive OPTIONAL fields remain at the current version. A bump is a coordinated change: this schema doc + `src/bin/cli.rs::format_structured_json` + the integration test's expected-keys set in `tests/decay_contract.rs` + `scripts/verify-decay.sh` field whitelist (when it's added; today the script greps 4 hardcoded field names) must all land in the same plan. Rationale: consumers (verify-decay.sh, future BL-010 daemon) tolerate additive growth but break on rename/remove/type-drift/invariant-drift. See docs/discussions/021-v0.8.0-bl-dependencies/conclusion.md and docs/plans/015-decay-operator-surface-hardening.md.
